verses.text: 'Sing, O barren,' for the desolate woman shall have more children than the married; enlarge thy tent. Fruitfulness follows barrenness. The Church, once small and afflicted, shall be filled and spread abroad.

'Sing, O barren,' for the desolate woman shall have more children than the married; enlarge thy tent. Fruitfulness follows barrenness. The Church, once small and afflicted, shall be filled and spread abroad.

'Sing, O barren,' for the desolate woman shall have more children than the married; enlarge thy tent. Fruitfulness follows barrenness. The Church, once small and afflicted, shall be filled and spread abroad.
Fear not; 'thy Maker is thine husband; the LORD of hosts is his name,' who calls back the forsaken wife. God reclaims His own. He woos and weds the people who once seemed cast off.
Fear not; 'thy Maker is thine husband; the LORD of hosts is his name,' who calls back the forsaken wife. God reclaims His own. He woos and weds the people who once seemed cast off.
Fear not; 'thy Maker is thine husband; the LORD of hosts is his name,' who calls back the forsaken wife. God reclaims His own. He woos and weds the people who once seemed cast off.
'For a small moment have I forsaken thee; but with great mercies will I gather thee.' The desertion was brief. God's anger is momentary, but His mercy is vast and lasting.
'For a small moment have I forsaken thee; but with great mercies will I gather thee.' The desertion was brief. God's anger is momentary, but His mercy is vast and lasting.
As He swore after Noah's flood, so now: 'the mountains shall depart... but my kindness shall not depart from thee.' His covenant is unshakable. God's love is surer than the everlasting hills.
As He swore after Noah's flood, so now: 'the mountains shall depart... but my kindness shall not depart from thee.' His covenant is unshakable. God's love is surer than the everlasting hills.
To the afflicted city He promises foundations of sapphires and gates of carbuncles, a glory beyond her storms. Beauty replaces ruin. God rebuilds His people more splendidly than before.
To the afflicted city He promises foundations of sapphires and gates of carbuncles, a glory beyond her storms. Beauty replaces ruin. God rebuilds His people more splendidly than before.
'All thy children shall be taught of the LORD,' established in righteousness, far from oppression. Peace and instruction abound. The people God teaches dwell secure in His righteousness.
'All thy children shall be taught of the LORD,' established in righteousness, far from oppression. Peace and instruction abound. The people God teaches dwell secure in His righteousness.

'No weapon that is formed against thee shall prosper'; this safety is 'the heritage of the servants of the LORD.' Their defence is sure. Every attack on God's people fails, for their security is His promise.
